resources.string: Local Telegram spam filter for LSPosed
Do not apply global rules to this chat
One rule per line. Available prefixes: text:, caption:, button:, sender:, chat:
One Java regex per line. Prefixes mean the same thing as in keyword rules.
Exclusions run first; a matched message is always kept visible.

Each input box now has a fixed target, so you do not need prefixes like text:, sender:, or chat: anymore. "Any field" checks message text, captions, buttons, sender names, chat names, and matching IDs together. "Sender" rules affect all messages from that sender. "Chat" rules affect every message in that chat.

This window only affects the current chat. Each input box now has a fixed target, so you do not need prefixes like text:, sender:, or chat: anymore. If you fill the "Chat" section with something that matches the current chat name, this is close to filtering the entire current chat.
When these rules match, the hide, collapse, or debug-mark action above will run.
These rules always keep messages visible
Exclusions win before filter rules. Use them as a whitelist for admins, notices, or specific chat names.
Choose whether you are editing filter rules or always-keep rules, then choose the field to check. Switching choices keeps what you typed.
This editor only affects the current chat. Choose filter or always-keep, then choose the field to check. Switching choices keeps what you typed.
One rule per line. This box only checks %1$s. No prefix is needed here.
One Java regex per line. This box only checks %1$s. No prefix is needed here.
There are no filter rules here yet.
There are no whitelist rules here yet.
message text, captions, buttons, senders, chat names, or matching IDs
When this matches, that message triggers filtering.
When this matches, messages from that sender all trigger filtering.
When this matches, every message in that chat triggers filtering.
When this matches, that message is always kept visible.
When this matches, messages from that sender are always kept visible.
When this matches, every message in that chat is always kept visible.
This window is already scoped to a single chat, so if the chat name matches, this is close to matching the entire chat.
Added a rule for this message
This message has no text to extract

GramSieve first requests root access to read the LSPosed private database in read-only mode and confirm module enablement and Telegram scope. If that fails, it falls back to installed-package detection. The database is never modified.
Detection only confirms that an app package is installed. It cannot confirm that the module is enabled in LSPosed, scoped to Telegram, or that a related feature is turned on.
LSPosed enablement and Telegram scope were confirmed from the database in read-only mode. Module-specific feature switches still cannot be confirmed.
Reading LSPosed module state in read-only mode…
LSPosed database read-only access succeeded. GramSieve: %1$s. Conflicts now include only modules that are enabled and scoped to Telegram.
Read-only root access was unavailable. Falling back to installed-package detection.
The LSPosed private database was not found. Falling back to installed-package detection.
The read-only LSPosed query timed out. Falling back to installed-package detection.
The LSPosed private database could not be read. Falling back to installed-package detection.
No known conflicting module packages detected.
No overlapping feature group was detected among the installed packages.
No overlapping feature group was detected among modules confirmed active for Telegram.
Telegram menu and cell UI collision
Multiple modules can remove the same delete update or short-circuit Telegram storage. Choose one anti-recall owner; otherwise GramSieve may miss recall events or local deletion may be blocked.
Both modules can capture the old message before Telegram writes an edit. Duplicate versions and duplicate menu entries are possible.
These modules write the same FileLoadOperation fields. Values do not stack; the last callback wins. Keep only one download accelerator.
Telegami and TeleVip can both replace SecretMediaViewer behavior. Keep Secret Media handling enabled in only one of them.
The modules target the same no-forwards and save checks. This is usually redundant, but it increases compatibility and diagnosis risk. Keep one owner.
The modules can short-circuit the same sponsored-message paths. Keep one ad-blocking implementation to reduce null and version-compatibility risk.
Several modules can hide Stories while Telegram Tweaks may move them. Keep Stories management in one module.
Telegami and TeleVip can both intercept read, typing, online, and story-view requests. Keep each privacy behavior enabled in only one module.
Multiple modules inject Telegram chat menus or decorate message cells. Duplicate entries or overwritten visual markers may appear.
